name: Parallel Reflectors — Sedimentary Sequence
description: Parallel to sub-parallel reflectors consistent with layered sedimentary sequence.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Parallel seismic reflectors are consistent with a stratified {{entity}}. Reflector continuity and amplitude indicate depositional layering with acoustic impedance contrasts.
additionalRecommendations: Velocity analysis required for depth conversion
Amplitude extraction along key horizons recommended

name: Strong Basal Reflector — Basement Contact
description: Strong laterally continuous basal reflection consistent with sediment-basement contact.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Strong basal reflector is consistent with the {{entity}} surface — high acoustic impedance contrast at sediment-basement contact.
additionalRecommendations: Tie to borehole for depth calibration
Compare basement depth map with gravity modelling results

name: Reflector Terminations — Fault
description: Abrupt termination and/or offset of reflectors consistent with faulting.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Reflector terminations and lateral offsets are consistent with a {{entity}}. Displacement magnitude and geometry require horizon flattening for kinematic analysis.
additionalRecommendations: Measure throw on key horizons to constrain fault kinematics

name: Velocity Model Dependency Warning
description: All depth conversions and structural interpretations are dependent on the velocity model.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Processing assumption: depth conversion of seismic reflectors is velocity-model dependent. Without well calibration, depth uncertainty is typically ±5–15%. Lateral velocity variations introduce additional structural ambiguity.
additionalRecommendations: Tie to nearest well with sonic log for velocity calibration
Perform sensitivity analysis on velocity model

name: Multiple Attenuation Required
description: Marine or near-surface multiples may be misinterpreted as primary geological reflectors.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Uncertainty warning: marine seismic data requires robust multiple attenuation before structural interpretation. Water-bottom multiples at twice the water depth are commonly mistaken for sub-seafloor geology.
additionalRecommendations: Apply SRME (Surface Related Multiple Elimination) before interpretation
Verify suspected geological reflectors are not at multiple periods of shallower events

name: Converging Reflectors — Fold Structure
description: Reflectors that converge or thicken laterally consistent with fold geometry.

conclusion.hypothesisStatement: Reflector convergence and lateral thickening are consistent with a {{entity}}. Isopach mapping of key horizons recommended to characterize fold geometry.
additionalRecommendations: Map fold hinge geometry in 3D if adequate line spacing
Consider structural restoration to verify kinematics
